title: Root Word Raccoon
short_description: Snap word-parts together — a prefix, a root, a suffix — to build the word that matches the clue. un + break + able = unbreakable!
description: Rocky the raccoon collects word-parts and builds bigger words out of them. Read the clue — 'not happy', 'to play it again', 'able to not be broken' — then tap the right prefix, root and suffix tiles to snap the word together and check it. It teaches morphology, the nuts and bolts of vocabulary: how prefixes like un-, re- and pre- change meaning, how suffixes like -ing, -ful and -able change how a word is used, and how roots carry the core idea. Grade bands ramp from adding a suffix to a root, to adding a prefix, to full prefix-root-suffix builds with Greek and Latin roots. Correct builds and streaks earn bonus points. A free online prefixes, suffixes, roots and word-building language game for kids in grades 1–5, feeding your score and GP on OnlyGrins.
controls: Mouse / tap prefix, root and suffix tiles to build the word, then Check.
